items.excerpt: Dragon Age lead writer David Gaider condemns generative AI in game development as a 'virulent plague,' saying BioWare's own attempts at AI dialogue were soulless and that executives keep pushing tech that isn't ready for prime time.

The team behind an unofficial Donkey Kong 64 PC recompilation publicly dunks on a rival project for 'vibe coding' its way through development with AI assistance rather than proper reverse-engineering craft.
A new Sonic ARG asks players to hand over personal data that would be used to train AI models, igniting significant backlash from fans over consent and data harvesting in a beloved game franchise.

Genshin Impact creator miHoYo quietly launched BSide: Olivia Lin on Steam, a free generative-AI desktop companion built around a piano-student character who plays uploaded MIDI files, responds to letters, and adapts conversations over time.

CD Projekt Red joint CEO Michal Nowakowski says fully AI-generated games are coming and he's seen studios prototyping them, but doubts flooding the market with AI-first titles built in weeks is a path worth following.
